name: Lucas Valbuena
bio:
Lucas Valbuena is the visionary founder of ZeroLeaks, a pioneering venture at the nexus of AI security and open-source transparency. At just 16 years old, this Spanish developer has rapidly established himself as a significant figure in the tech community, amassing a substantial following through his incisive work. His journey is defined by a unique blend of deep technical curiosity and a strong ethical stance, leading him to deconstruct and analyze the proprietary inner workings of popular AI coding assistants. This investigative drive culminated in his flagship GitHub project, a massive repository of reverse-engineered system prompts, which serves as a critical transparency resource for developers and researchers worldwide. It is from this foundational work on exposure and analysis that the mission for ZeroLeaks was born.

ZeroLeaks emerges as the natural, product-oriented extension of Lucas’s core philosophy. The platform directly addresses the vulnerabilities he meticulously documents, offering tools to test and secure AI applications against prompt injection and data leakage. His technical writings and projects, such as Better-Clawd and Zero Calendar, consistently emphasize building open-source, telemetry-free alternatives to closed systems, championing user agency and security. This principled approach to development—where understanding a system’s flaws is the first step in fortifying it—forms the bedrock of ZeroLeaks’ value proposition. Lucas operates with the conviction that true security and innovation in the AI era require both radical transparency and robust, accessible tools to safeguard it.

Looking forward, Lucas Valbuena represents a new archetype of founder: a digital-native investigator turned builder. His work, which also explores cutting-edge intersections like AI-governed blockchain oracles, demonstrates a forward-thinking approach to decentralized systems and automation. Despite his youth, his output reflects a mature focus on impactful, foundational problems in the AI stack. Through ZeroLeaks and his prolific open-source contributions, Lucas is not just building a company; he is actively shaping the practices and ethical standards for a generation of developers navigating an increasingly complex and opaque AI-integrated world. His career narrative is one of leveraging curiosity into expertise, and expertise into tangible solutions for the global developer ecosystem.

timeline.event: Lucas Valbuena, a 16-year-old developer from Spain, gains significant visibility on GitHub with the repository 'system-prompts-and-models-of-ai-tools', which becomes a major resource in the open AI tooling community
Releases 'better-clawd', an open-source alternative to Claude Code, emphasizing performance and vendor flexibility
Publishes 'zero-calendar', an AI-native calendar application, showcasing product-thinking and initiative to build alternatives to existing services
Develops 'market-ai-resolution', an ambitious project exploring rule-based AI reasoning for prediction markets at the intersection of DeFi and AI
Creates 'awesome-solana-ai', demonstrating interest in the convergence of AI and blockchain/crypto within the Solana ecosystem
Establishes ZeroLeaks, focusing on AI security and vulnerabilities, particularly prompt injection and extraction attacks
Publishes blog content positioning himself as an investigator/reverse-engineer of AI systems, sharing proprietary system prompts and security findings
Builds public profile as a security-conscious builder, testing AI tool vulnerabilities and developing solutions through ZeroLeaks platform
Advances work on AI security tools, focusing on prompt leakage prevention and secure AI application testing methodologies
Continues development of pragmatic open-source developer tools with emphasis on telemetry-free, efficient workflows
Maintains active GitHub presence with 3,543 followers and 9 public repositories, establishing influence in AI/ML and developer tooling communities

key_contributions.title: system-prompts-and-models-of-ai-tools
key_contributions.description: A massively popular repository curating and 'leaking' the internal system prompts and model configurations from major AI coding assistants (Cursor, Warp, v0, etc.). It serves as a critical transparency and educational resource for the AI developer community.

key_contributions.title: Better-Clawd
key_contributions.description: An open-source, high-performance alternative to Claude Code, designed with a focus on speed, vendor flexibility, and a commitment to no telemetry or lock-in. It demonstrates full-stack product development in the AI-powered developer tools space.

key_contributions.title: Zero Calendar
key_contributions.description: An 'AI-native' calendar application built with modern web technologies (TypeScript, Next.js). The project explores integrating AI deeply into productivity and scheduling workflows, moving beyond simple chatbot interfaces.

key_contributions.title: Market AI Resolution
key_contributions.description: A conceptual project proposing to replace traditional oracle voting mechanisms in blockchain-based prediction markets with 'rule-based AI reasoning.' It explores novel governance, trust, and automation models at the intersection of DeFi and AI.

key_contributions.title: AI Security Research & ZeroLeaks Philosophy
key_contributions.description: A body of work, documented through his blog, focused on identifying and mitigating vulnerabilities in LLM applications, particularly prompt injection and extraction attacks. This research underpins the mission of his company, ZeroLeaks, which aims to build tools for testing and securing AI systems.

quotes.text: The advantage of being curious early

executive_summary.career_arc: Lucas Valbuena began his public career in early 2024 by publishing a massive repository of reverse-engineered system prompts from AI coding assistants, which quickly became a cornerstone resource for transparency in the AI community. This foundational work established his reputation and led to the creation of ZeroLeaks, a platform addressing the very vulnerabilities he exposed. His trajectory evolved from investigator and reverse-engineer to builder, developing open-source alternatives like Better-Clawd and Zero Calendar while exploring AI-blockchain convergence projects.
executive_summary.current_focus: He is advancing ZeroLeaks as a platform for AI security testing, focusing on prompt leakage prevention and secure AI application methodologies, while continuing to develop open-source tools and share findings on AI system vulnerabilities.
executive_summary.industry_significance: Valbuena represents a new archetype of founder—a digital-native investigator turned builder—who is shaping ethical standards and security practices in the AI-integrated world by championing transparency and robust, accessible tools for developers.
key_facts: Founder of ZeroLeaks, an AI security platform focused on testing vulnerabilities like prompt injection and data leakage.
Gained viral recognition with a GitHub repository curating reverse-engineered system prompts from major AI coding assistants, amassing over 135,000 stars.
Advocates for radical transparency, open-source alternatives, and a 'no telemetry, no lock-in' philosophy in developer tools.
Built projects like Better-Clawd and Zero Calendar, demonstrating full-stack product development in AI-powered applications.
Explores cutting-edge intersections of AI and blockchain, such as AI-governed prediction markets and Solana-based AI tools.
Operates as a bootstrapped, independent developer with no traditional venture funding, relying on community influence and open-source contributions.
Maintains a strong GitHub presence with 3,543 followers and multiple popular repositories, establishing authority in AI/ML and security circles.
Employs a hacker-investigator ethos, treating AI systems as puzzles to deconstruct and secure through hands-on research and tool-building.
executive_summary.one_liner: Lucas Valbuena is a 16-year-old Spanish developer and founder of ZeroLeaks, who has gained significant influence in the AI security and open-source communities by reverse-engineering proprietary AI systems and building tools to promote transparency and security.

questions.question: Your 'system-prompts-and-models-of-ai-tools' repository became a massive transparency resource with over 135,000 stars. How did the process of reverse-engineering and publishing those proprietary prompts directly lead to the founding of ZeroLeaks? Was there a specific vulnerability you uncovered that made you realize this needed to be a product, not just a GitHub repo?
questions.why_this_question: It connects his most famous work directly to his company's origin, moving beyond generic 'how did you start' to the specific technical catalyst.
questions.expected_insight: A detailed story about discovering a specific, exploitable pattern across multiple AI tools that revealed a systemic security gap, prompting the shift from researcher to builder of defensive tools.

questions.question: Before ZeroLeaks, you built 'better-clawd' as an open-source, telemetry-free alternative to Claude Code. That project required deep product thinking. How did the experience of building a full-stack AI developer tool shape your approach to founding and building ZeroLeaks as a company?
questions.why_this_question: It probes the transition from open-source project maintainer to founder, using a concrete prior project as the reference point.
questions.expected_insight: Insights into lessons learned about user needs, performance trade-offs in AI tools, and how the 'no telemetry, no lock-in' philosophy from better-clawd translates to a security-focused SaaS business model.

questions.question: In your blog, you position yourself as an investigator of AI systems. For ZeroLeaks, what were the key architectural decisions in designing a platform that can reliably test for prompt injection and extraction across different LLM providers and application frameworks? How do you balance comprehensiveness with performance?
questions.why_this_question: It references his stated identity and asks for specific technical choices behind his main product, avoiding generic 'how does it work' questions.
questions.expected_insight: Deep technical details on the testing engine—whether it uses symbolic execution, fuzzing, or a library of known attack patterns—and how it interfaces with various APIs and frameworks like LangChain or LlamaIndex.

questions.question: Your 'market-ai-resolution' project explores rule-based AI reasoning for blockchain oracles. That's a highly specific technical intersection. How does your research into formal methods and deterministic AI systems for DeFi influence the way you architect tests for non-deterministic, conversational LLM vulnerabilities at ZeroLeaks?
questions.why_this_question: It connects a seemingly niche side project to his main work, forcing a synthesis of ideas across different technical domains he's explored.
questions.expected_insight: An explanation of how thinking about verifiable, rule-based systems in one domain informs creating rigorous, repeatable security tests in another, potentially revealing a unique cross-disciplinary approach to AI security.

questions.question: Your work rests on a tension: you've built a business (ZeroLeaks) by exposing the vulnerabilities of the very AI tools (like those from Cursor, Warp) that many developers, including yourself, use to build things. Is this a sustainable, symbiotic relationship, or an inherently adversarial one? What's your long-term view on this dynamic?
questions.why_this_question: It directly confronts the core philosophical and business conflict in his work, referencing the specific tools he's analyzed.
questions.expected_insight: A nuanced take on the ethics and economics of security research, and whether he sees ZeroLeaks as a necessary immune system for the AI ecosystem or a force that will push vendors toward greater inherent security and openness.

questions.question: You have a clear stance against telemetry and vendor lock-in, as seen in 'better-clawd'. In an AI security context, does this philosophy extend to believing that the most secure AI applications will necessarily be open-source and self-hosted? Is there a future where you trust a proprietary, cloud-based AI system to be secure?
questions.why_this_question: It takes a specific principle from his past projects and applies it rigorously to his core domain of AI security, testing for a potentially contrarian take.
questions.expected_insight: A defense or evolution of his open-source ethos within the security realm, possibly acknowledging practical trade-offs or arguing that transparency is non-negotiable for true security assurance.

questions.question: Your 'awesome-solana-ai' repo curates resources at the AI/blockchain intersection. Have specific collaborations or conversations within that niche community influenced the roadmap for ZeroLeaks? For instance, are you seeing unique AI security threats emerge in on-chain or agentic applications?
questions.why_this_question: It identifies a specific community he's engaged with (Solana AI devs) and asks for concrete influence on his product, moving beyond generic 'community' questions.
questions.expected_insight: Examples of novel vulnerability classes discussed in web3/AI circles (e.g., adversarial attacks on transaction-generating agents) that are shaping ZeroLeaks' feature development.

questions.question: With 3,500+ GitHub followers gained largely from one repo, you have a highly engaged but specific audience of developers interested in AI tooling internals. How are you leveraging that community not just for distribution, but for collaborative threat research to fuel ZeroLeaks' detection capabilities?
questions.why_this_question: It acknowledges the scale and origin of his community and asks for a specific, non-obvious mechanism of collaboration (co-research).
questions.expected_insight: Details on structured feedback loops, bounty programs for novel prompt injections, or open-source contributions to the ZeroLeaks testing corpus from his follower base.

questions.question: Zero Calendar is an AI-native app, and ZeroLeaks secures AI apps. Do you see these paths converging? Is the long-term vision for ZeroLeaks to evolve into or inspire a suite of secure-by-default, AI-native development frameworks or primitives?
questions.why_this_question: It connects two of his major projects to speculate on a unified future vision, showing an understanding of his broader portfolio.
questions.expected_insight: A vision for shifting from finding vulnerabilities to defining and enabling a secure architecture for AI applications, potentially through open-source frameworks or developer protocols.

questions.question: You've explored AI for prediction market resolution. Looking ahead, do you believe the biggest AI security challenges will shift from prompt leakage in today's chat applications to securing autonomous, economically incentivized AI agents? How is ZeroLeaks preparing for that potential future?
questions.why_this_question: It uses his most forward-looking research project ('market-ai-resolution') as a lens to ask about the future of his core business, demonstrating a deep read of his interests.
questions.expected_insight: Predictions on the evolution of AI threats as systems become more agentic and financially capable, and early R&D at ZeroLeaks into testing the security of goal-driven, multi-step AI agents.
